description.p: Plotex is an interactive graphical scientific plotting and graphing package — a fork of Veusz 4.2 by Jeremy Sanders. Designed to produce publication-ready PDF, SVG, EMF and bitmap output. Reads data from text, CSV, HDF5 and FITS files. Provides a command line and Python scripting interface to its plotting facilities and can also be used as a Python plotting module.

Plotex extends Veusz with new plot types relevant to medical and statistical research: Kaplan-Meier survival curves, ROC curves with confidence bands, Bland-Altman plots, violin plots, pie charts, Pareto charts, QQ plots, ridgeline plots, heatmaps, polar trending and bracket annotations. It also adds 12+ plot themes (Classic, Publication, ggplot2, Seaborn, Economist, BMJ, GraphPad Prism, Solarized…), 6 scientific color palettes, a modernized ribbon UI, command palette, and a hardened safe-mode with HMAC-signed bytecode cache.

Audit fixes: security (HMAC cache, hardened safe-mode AST validator, forbidden_attrs blacklist, ENVIRON whitelist), threading (painter try/finally, loader timeouts, document snapshot under read lock), UX (Preferences Cancel actually cancels, fitdialog re-entrancy fix), settings (cycle detection, type-strict normalisers), build coherence. 61 fixes, 107 regression tests.
Hotfix: loader hang on Windows when opening files from protected directories (e.g. Program Files). Bytecode cache moved to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Plotex\cache.
Initial public release of the Plotex fork from Veusz 4.2.
